title: sideritis libanotica subsp. kurdica
intro: Sideritis libanotica subsp. kurdica, also known as Kurdish mountain tea, is a subspecies of the Sideritis libanotica plant, a member of the mint family. This perennial herb is native to the mountainous regions of Kurdistan, and its leaves and flowers have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are beneficial for reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting against cell damage. Sideritis libanotica subsp. kurdica can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, and it is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
